title: Loss Prevention Officer | AYANA Komodo
locations_derived: Labuan Bajo, East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia
description_text:
AYANA Hospitality is seeking a diligent and proactive Loss Prevention Officer to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will be integral in ensuring the safety and security of our guests, staff, and property by preventing theft and mitigating risks associated with incidents. Your keen observation skills and ability to respond effectively to emergencies will be key to maintaining a secure environment.
Key Responsibilities
Conduct regular patrols of the property to monitor and assess security needs and address any anomalies.
Respond promptly to security alarms, incidents, and guest inquiries while providing a professional and calm presence.
Monitor surveillance cameras and report any suspicious activities or concerns to the management.
Assist in investigating incidents (theft, vandalism, etc.), documenting findings, and reporting them in accordance with procedures.
Collaborate with security teams and other departments to ensure compliance with all safety and security protocols.
Maintain accurate records of daily activities, incidents, and irregularities.
Ensure effective communication with guests and staff regarding safety procedures, fostering a secure and friendly atmosphere.
High school diploma or equivalent; additional education in a related field is a plus.
At least 1-2 years of experience in a security or loss prevention role, preferably in the hospitality industry.
Strong observational and communication skills, with the ability to interact effectively with guests and staff.
Proficiency in English, both written and verbal.
Ability to remain calm and assess situations effectively in high-pressure circumstances.
Basic knowledge of security systems and safety protocols.
Willingness to work flexible hours, including nights, weekends, and holidays.
ai_remote_location: Labuan Bajo, East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia
ai_remote_location_derived: Labuan Bajo, East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia
ai_core_responsibilities: The Loss Prevention Officer will conduct regular patrols to monitor security needs and respond to incidents. They will also assist in investigating incidents and maintain accurate records of daily activities.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a high school diploma and 1-2 years of experience in a security or loss prevention role, preferably in hospitality. Strong observational and communication skills, along with a basic knowledge of security systems, are essential.

linkedin_org_description: AYANA, named after the Sanskrit word for ‘a place of refuge' is a globally respected owner and creator of iconic destinations. We believe hospitality should nurture the wellbeing of our guests and communities. That travel should bring joy, invigorate the senses and enliven the mind. And that our success depends on achieving harmony with self, others and nature. We exist to craft iconic destinations and voyages that provide exceptional service and profound inspiration to our global travelers. For every guest, a place of refuge. That is our pledge.

title: Business Development Associate (Full-Time)
description_text:
TGA (Tennis Golf Athletics) is a growing youth sports organization bringing high-quality, character-building sports programs to schools and communities across Marin and Sonoma County. We work with kids, schools, and families — and we’re expanding fast.
We’re looking for a full-time Business Development Associate who wants to grow with the business, help expand programs, and build real experience in sports management, youth programs, and operations.
This role is ideal for a recent graduate or early-career professional interested in sports, children, community programs, or entrepreneurship.
The Role
This is a full-time, in-person role (40 hours/week) focused on growing TGA’s footprint across Marin and Sonoma County.
You’ll work closely with ownership to:
Build relationships with schools, PTAs, community organizations, and sports facilities 
Support new school partnerships and program launches 
Help grow enrollment and awareness of TGA programs 
Conduct site visits and represent TGA in the community 
Support expansion into new sports (tennis, golf, pickleball — and more coming) 
You do not need to play all the sports we offer. Experience with tennis, golf, or pickleball is a plus, but enthusiasm, professionalism, and follow-through matter most.
💰 Compensation
$25/hour (full-time, non-exempt) 
Commission opportunities in addition to hourly pay, tied to: 
New partnerships 
Enrollment growth 
Program expansions 
Clear performance expectations and regular feedback 
🚀 Growth Path
This is a growth-track role. Strong performance can lead to advancement into a General Manager position with increased responsibility over:
Operations 
People management 
Regional growth and strategy 
We’re looking for someone who wants to build, not just show up.
✅ Requirements (Please Read Carefully)
Must live in Marin or Sonoma County 
Must have a reliable car and valid driver’s license 
Must be available Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM–5:00 PM 
Comfortable traveling locally between schools and partner sites 
Professional communication and strong organizational skills
🙌 Ideal Candidate
Recent graduate or early-career professional 
Interested in sports, youth programs, education, or sports management 
Organized, reliable, and comfortable working independently 
Confident communicating with adults (school staff, parents, partners) 
Excited about growth, responsibility, and leadership opportunities
🎁 Benefits
$25/hour + commission opportunities 
Paid time off and paid holidays (accrual-based) 
Mileage reimbursement for work-related travel outside of the normal commute
Growth opportunity into a General Manager role 
Mentorship and hands-on business experience 
Mission-driven work in youth sports and education
Employment Type: Full-time, hourly, non-exempt
ai_core_responsibilities: The Business Development Associate will focus on growing TGA’s footprint across Marin and Sonoma County by building relationships with schools and community organizations. They will support new school partnerships, program launches, and help grow enrollment and awareness of TGA programs.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must live in Marin or Sonoma County and have a reliable car and valid driver’s license. They should be available Monday to Friday from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM and possess strong organizational and communication skills.
linkedin_org_slogan: The #1 Youth Sports Provider for Tennis, Golf, & Athletics
linkedin_org_locations: 606 Columbus Ave, New York, NY 10024, US
linkedin_org_description: TGA combines athletics, academics and life lessons to create fun and engaging youth programs.  We incorporate station-based athletic skill development activities, programs explore educational concepts through sports, and key life lessons including teamwork, sportsmanship, perseverance, and leadership.

title: Direct Support Professional
description_text:
Creed Medical is looking for compassionate and motivated individuals to join our team as Direct Support Professionals (DSP). In this rewarding role, you will provide essential support to individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities, helping them lead fulfilling lives. As a DSP, you will assist clients in daily living activities, promote independence, and encourage social engagement in the community. Your dedication and care will play a pivotal role in enhancing their quality of life and enabling them to reach their personal goals. If you have a passion for making a difference and empowering others, we invite you to apply and become a valued member of our team at Creed Medical.

Key Responsibilities
Assist individuals with daily living tasks such as personal care, meal preparation, and medication management.
Develop and implement individualized support plans to help clients achieve their goals.
Encourage and facilitate participation in social and community activities.
Document daily activities and progress, maintaining accurate records for each individual.
Ensure a safe and supportive environment for all clients.
Collaborate with families and healthcare professionals to optimize care and support strategies.
High school diploma or equivalent required; experience in a similar role is preferred.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills with a compassionate attitude.
Ability to work independently as well as part of a team.
Valid driver's license and reliable transportation for community outings.
Willingness to work flexible hours, including nights and weekends as needed.
Certification in CPR and First Aid is preferred or must be obtained shortly after hiring.
Flexible Schedule.
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Direct Support Professional, you will assist individuals with daily living tasks and develop individualized support plans. You will also encourage social engagement and document daily activities to enhance the quality of life for clients.
ai_requirements_summary: A high school diploma or equivalent is required, with experience in a similar role preferred. Strong communication skills and a compassionate attitude are essential, along with a valid driver's license and willingness to work flexible hours.

title: Direct support professional
description_text:
Join the dedicated team at Creed Medical as a Direct Support Professional (DSP), where you will have the unique opportunity to empower individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities in their day-to-day lives. In this rewarding role, you will provide critical support and assistance to clients, promoting their independence and enhancing their quality of life. Your responsibilities will include helping with daily living activities, fostering community engagement, and encouraging personal development. At Creed Medical, we value compassion, respect, and commitment, and we are looking for individuals who share our passion for making a meaningful difference in the lives of those we serve.

Key Responsibilities
Assist clients with daily living tasks such as bathing, dressing, and meal preparation.
Implement and follow individualized support plans to help clients achieve their personal goals.
Encourage social interactions and community participation to enhance overall well-being.
Document client progress and maintain accurate records of services provided.
Create and maintain a safe, clean, and supportive environment.
Collaborate with the care team to ensure a holistic approach to client support.
High school diploma or equivalent is required; further education in a related field is preferred.
Experience as a Direct Support Professional or in a similar role is a plus.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills to effectively interact with clients and their families.
Understanding of applicable health and safety regulations.
Valid driver’s license and reliable transportation for client outings.
Willingness to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and some holidays.
CPR and First Aid certification preferred or must be obtained shortly after being hired.
Flexible Schedule.
ai_core_responsibilities: Assist clients with daily living tasks and implement individualized support plans. Encourage social interactions and document client progress to enhance overall well-being.
ai_requirements_summary: A high school diploma or equivalent is required, with further education in a related field preferred. Experience as a Direct Support Professional or in a similar role is a plus, along with strong communication skills and a valid driver's license.

title: Direct support Professionals
description_text:
Creed Medical is seeking dedicated and compassionate Direct Support Professionals (DSPs) to join our team and provide exceptional care to individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities. As a DSP, you will have the profound opportunity to positively impact the lives of those we serve by assisting them in achieving their goals, enhancing their independence, and promoting their overall well-being. In this role, you will support individuals in their daily activities, encourage community involvement, and help create a nurturing environment that fosters personal growth. If you are a caring individual with a passion for advocacy and empowerment, we would love to hear from you!

Key Responsibilities
Assist individuals with activities of daily living, including personal care, meal planning, and household management.
Implement and follow personalized support plans while encouraging independence.
Engage individuals in recreational and community activities to enhance social skills and integration.
Monitor and document individual progress, communicating any changes to the care team.
Provide companionship, emotional support, and a positive environment for individuals.
Work collaboratively with other staff and families to ensure holistic care.
High school diploma or equivalent; further education in social work, psychology, or a related field is advantageous.
Experience in a similar role or working with individuals with disabilities preferred.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with a patient and empathetic demeanor.
Commitment to confidentiality and adherence to privacy regulations (HIPAA).
Valid driver's license and the ability to transport individuals when needed.
Availability to work flexible hours, including nights, weekends, and holidays.
CPR and First Aid certification preferred or required within the first month of employment.
Flexible Schedule.
ai_core_responsibilities: Direct Support Professionals will assist individuals with daily activities and implement personalized support plans. They will also engage individuals in community activities and provide emotional support.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a high school diploma or equivalent, with experience in a similar role preferred. Strong communication skills and a commitment to confidentiality are essential.

title: Direct support Professional
description_text:
At Creed Medical, we are committed to empowering individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities through personalized support and compassionate care. We are currently seeking a Direct Support Professional (DSP) who is dedicated to making a meaningful impact in the lives of those we serve. As a DSP, you will provide essential assistance with daily activities, help individuals achieve their personal goals, and foster a positive and inclusive community environment.

Key Responsibilities
Support individuals in daily living tasks, including personal care, meal preparation, and household activities.
Encourage social interactions and participation in community activities to enhance individual well-being.
Implement and follow individualized support plans aimed at promoting independence and self-advocacy.
Maintain accurate documentation of care provided and track progress in achieving personal goals.
Create and maintain a safe and nurturing environment for individuals at all times.
Collaborate with family members and interdisciplinary teams to ensure comprehensive support.
High school diploma or GED required; certification in Human Services or related field is preferred.
Experience in direct support or caregiving roles is highly desirable.
Strong communication skills and a compassionate approach to caregiving.
Ability to respect and maintain confidentiality in all aspects of care.
Valid driver's license with reliable transportation for community activities.
Willingness to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays as needed.
Current CPR and First Aid certification preferred, or must be obtained after employment.
Flexible Schedule.
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Direct Support Professional, you will assist individuals with daily living tasks and help them achieve personal goals. You will also foster a positive community environment and maintain accurate documentation of care provided.
ai_requirements_summary: A high school diploma or GED is required, with a preference for certification in Human Services or a related field. Experience in direct support or caregiving roles is highly desirable, along with strong communication skills and a compassionate approach.

title: Direct Support Professional
description_text:
Creed Medical is seeking a compassionate and dedicated Direct Support Professional (DSP) to join our committed team. In this fulfilling role, you will assist individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities to lead empowered and independent lives. As a DSP, you will provide support with daily activities, encouraging personal growth and facilitating community involvement. Your responsibilities will primarily include helping individuals with personal care tasks, skill development, and emotional support, creating a positive and inclusive environment. If you are looking to make a meaningful impact and share your compassionate spirit with those in need, consider joining Creed Medical as a Direct Support Professional.

Responsibilities
Assist individuals with daily living tasks such as bathing, dressing, meal planning, and attending appointments.
Provide companionship and emotional support, fostering positive relationships.
Engage individuals in community activities, promoting social skills and inclusion.
Implement and monitor individual care plans, documenting progress and needs accurately.
Ensure a safe environment by adhering to health and safety standards, and addressing any immediate concerns.
Work collaboratively with other staff members and families to advocate for the needs of individuals.
High school diploma or equivalent; relevant experience in social services or healthcare preferred.
Experience in direct care or similar roles is a strong plus.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to demonstrate empathy and patience in various circumstances.
Valid driver’s license and reliable transportation for community activities.
Availability to work flexible hours, including nights and weekends.
Current CPR and First Aid certification is preferred or required to obtain upon hiring.
Flexible Schedule.
ai_core_responsibilities: Assist individuals with daily living tasks and provide emotional support. Engage individuals in community activities and implement individual care plans.
ai_requirements_summary: A high school diploma or equivalent is required, with relevant experience in social services or healthcare preferred. Candidates should demonstrate excellent communication skills and have a valid driver's license.

title: Apprentice Electrician - Mission Critical Data Center
description_text:
Department: National Accounts
Location: San Antonio, TX 
FLSA Status: (Non-Exempt / Full Time) $25 - $55 hour depending on experience PLUS Retention Bonuses and Per Diem $$$
_________________________________________________________________
Job Summary: 
Enterprise Electrical is seeking skilled and motivated Electricians of all levels — Apprentice electricians and Crew Support — to support a mission-critical Data Center project in San Antonio, TX. Candidates must have prior proven data center project experience and demonstrate a commitment to safety, quality, and teamwork. This is an excellent opportunity for professional electricians seeking steady hours, competitive pay, and strong travel support.
We take pride in being a positive place you can look forward to working at every day and we need a committed, experienced, self-starting individual to join our ranks.
________________________________________________________________
Open Roles Compensation Structure:
Entry-level Apprentice (Crew Support): $15 – $20 per hour
Apprentice 1 (requires over 1 year of verified experience): $22 – $25 per hour
Apprentice 2 (requires over 2 years of verified experience): $26 – $29 per hour
________________________________________________________________
Incentives and Project Perks:
Daily Per Diem: $120 (Paid daily, 7 days a week)
Retention Bonus: An additional $5.00 per hour - paid as a retention bonus for this project
(All overtime is calculated strictly from your base hourly rate, as required by wage and hour guidelines. The retention bonus will still be paid for all hours worked, but it does not multiply during overtime.)
Standard Schedule: 6/10s
Overtime: Available
Project Duration: 1 year+ with additional steady work
________________________________________________________________
An active electrical trade license is required to work.
A valid driver’s license or identification is necessary.
Reliable transportation is a must.
Experience in a commercial or industrial electrical setting for 1–3 years is essential, with a preference for those familiar with data centers or mission-critical environments.
A basic understanding of electrical systems, tools, materials, and safety practices is required.
Capability to read and interpret instructions, diagrams, and construction documents under supervision is expected.
A commitment to learning and adhering to NEC codes, OSHA standards, and Enterprise Electrical safety protocols is essential.
A strong work ethic, punctuality, and a readiness to travel and work full-time on-site are important.
Physical capabilities, including lifting, bending, climbing ladders, and working in confined spaces, are required.
A valid driver’s license and dependable transportation are necessary.
A positive attitude, willingness to learn, and ability to collaborate effectively as part of a team in a mission-critical environment are essential qualities.
________________________________________________________________
Preferred Qualifications
Previous experience in Mission Critical Data Center projects (including hyperscale, colocation, or critical infrastructure environments).
Active OSHA 10 or OSHA 30 certification.
CPR and First Aid certification.
Experience working with UPS systems, generators, switchgear, and grounding systems.
Ability to use basic hand tools, power tools, and electrical testing equipment effectively.
Familiarity with Procore, PlanGrid, or comparable project management software.
Completion of journeyman license coursework or a substantial number of hours towards achieving journeyman status. 
________________________________________________________________
Full-time employment opportunity
Work environment that emphasizes collaboration and teamwork
Health insurance coverage
Dental and Vision Insurance (offered by Enterprise)
Eligibility for 401K after 90 days of employment
Potential for Retention Bonuses
Paid Time Off (one week available following 90 days of employment)
Paid Holidays (8.5 days available immediately)
Attractive salary, stable work hours, and comprehensive travel support
Access to resources and opportunities for professional development
Ongoing travel opportunities as we enhance our data centers throughout the United States
Opportunities for career advancement
ai_core_responsibilities: Support a mission-critical Data Center project in San Antonio, TX. Ensure safety, quality, and teamwork while working on electrical systems.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have an active electrical trade license and experience in a commercial or industrial electrical setting for 1-3 years. A commitment to learning NEC codes and OSHA standards is essential.
linkedin_org_description: Electrical Contractor, specializing in design-build projects.

title: Senior Coach (Psychology / Coaching Background)
description_text:
Marriage Reset (MM Coaching Limited) helps men save their marriages through results-oriented online coaching - often succeeding where traditional counseling falls short. In under three years, we’ve achieved 10x growth and continue expanding rapidly, delivering exceptional outcomes for our clients every day.
How? We’ve focused on growth and the exceptional coaching for our clients and team members.
By creating a high-performance, positive environment we’ve helped thousands of clients with saving their marriage and achieving their full potential through direct support, coaching and consulting.
Hear From Our CEO:
Learn more about our mission and what drives us directly from our CEO, Marko Lamza:
https://www.loom.com/share/267f68d1f1164ebabe71aa137a1a93c8?sid=109628ff-329d-4024-85f4-722ca11aa1a5
Hear From Tom, One of Our Coaches:
https://www.loom.com/share/e397e88c8de145a2844f7155dafd16f3?sid=03a67540-7d52-4bec-aded-b66a7aa07bc1
Visit Our Website: 
https://marriagereset.com/
What It’s Like Working at MM Coaching Limited (Marriage Reset):
We’re growth-oriented, entrepreneurial, and ambitious. Our culture is built on supporting each team member to exceed their goals and make meaningful contributions to the company’s success.
Working with us means you’ll join a team of exceptionally talented individuals who are driven by results and innovation. We see our team members as partners, and we work together to raise standards and achieve excellence.
What We’re Looking For 
We are seeking a Senior Coach with deep, hands-on experience in relationship and marriage-focused coaching to support clients navigating high-stakes personal and emotional challenges.
This role is ideal for someone who has spent significant time coaching individuals or couples through marital conflict, emotional disconnection, trust breakdowns, separation, reconciliation, or long-term relationship rebuilding.
You are comfortable holding space for difficult conversations, offering direct yet compassionate guidance, and translating complex emotional dynamics into clear, actionable steps. This is not a surface-level coaching role — it requires maturity, emotional intelligence, and real-world exposure to relationship transformation work.

What You’ll Do 
Coaching & Client Support
Deliver high-impact 1:1 and group coaching sessions focused on marriage dynamics, emotional regulation, communication breakdowns, trust repair, and personal accountability
Guide clients through emotionally charged situations with clarity, structure, and proven frameworks
Help clients move from crisis and confusion to stability, leadership, and long-term behavioral change
Client Experience & Retention
Track client progress across emotionally complex journeys, identifying risks, resistance patterns, and breakthrough moments
Proactively intervene when clients struggle with consistency, emotional setbacks, or decision paralysis
Contribute insights to continuously improve coaching frameworks and client outcomes

Coaching Expertise
Certified coach (ICF, IFS, or similar), with demonstrated experience coaching around relationships, marriage, or long-term partnerships
Relevant Applied Experience
Proven background working with:
Marriage or relationship coaching
Couples, family systems, or individual clients navigating relationship crises
Emotional regulation, conflict resolution, accountability, and behavioral change
Emotional Maturity & Presence
Comfortable working with clients in high emotional distress
Able to balance empathy with firmness, challenge avoidance patterns, and maintain authority in difficult conversations
Communication Excellence
Strong verbal communication skills, able to explain complex emotional concepts in a grounded, practical way
Confident facilitating group discussions on sensitive topics
Operational Fit
Organized, reliable, and comfortable operating within structured programs
Available to work primarily between 1PM and 9PM EST
Work from Anywhere: Enjoy the freedom of a fully remote position, giving you the flexibility to work from wherever you feel most productive.
Commission Opportunities: Receive bonuses through commission on all additional purchases made by your clients.
Starting Salary: $40/hour
Ongoing Growth: Get access to free training, resources, and continuous learning opportunities to help you level up your coaching and professional skills.
ai_core_responsibilities: Deliver high-impact coaching sessions focused on marriage dynamics and emotional regulation. Guide clients through emotionally charged situations and track their progress across complex journeys.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have deep experience in relationship and marriage-focused coaching, with a proven background in navigating marital conflicts and emotional challenges. The role requires maturity, emotional intelligence, and the ability to facilitate difficult conversations.
linkedin_org_slogan: We help men improve and save their marriage without counseling, books or talking about the problems.
linkedin_org_description: We help men save their marriages, even when they are on the brink of divorce.

title: Manager - Merchandise (Commercial Kitchen Ware)
description_text:
Special Categories: Commercial Kitchen Ware

Key Responsibilities
Lead and manage merchandising and buying activities for assigned product categories, covering assortment planning, sourcing, pricing, and lifecycle management.
Conduct market, trend, and competitor analysis to identify commercial opportunities that drive sales growth, margin improvement, and category competitiveness.
Work closely with Category, Commercial, and Cross-functional teams to review assortments, identify gaps, and optimize product mix aligned with customer needs and business strategy.
Identify, evaluate, and manage suppliers and manufacturers, both local and international, ensuring capability in cost competitiveness, quality, capacity, and lead time.
Lead commercial negotiations with suppliers on pricing, MOQs, payment terms, rebates, and commercial conditions to achieve target margin and profitability.
Own costing, pricing strategy, and margin management, ensuring competitive retail pricing while meeting financial targets.
Manage buying forecasts, order planning, and replenishment alignment in collaboration with Supply Chain and Planning teams to ensure optimal stock availability and inventory efficiency.
Coordinate with QA and Regulatory teams to ensure products comply with relevant quality standards, safety requirements, and local regulations.
Monitor sales performance, stock movement, margin, and sell-through, and initiate corrective actions on assortment, pricing, or sourcing when required.
Support new product introduction and range changes, ensuring timely execution and alignment with commercial objectives.
Conduct supplier visits and factory assessments as required to support sourcing decisions and long-term supplier partnerships.
Perform additional tasks and strategic initiatives as assigned by senior management.
Bachelor's Degree required or related work.
7 - 10 experience with merchandising and supplier relationships.
Result orientation, Good planning and execution.
Teamwork & collaboration cross functionally.
Strong analytical skills and problemsolving.
Computer skills (MS office, advanced excel, Word).
Strong buying and merchandising expertise with hands-on experience in sourcing, negotiation, and supplier management.
Solid understanding of cost structure, pricing, margin optimization, and commercial trade-offs.
Experience in assortment planning, SKU rationalization, and category performance management.
Data-driven mindset with ability to analyze sales, margin, stock, and supplier performance.
Strong negotiation, stakeholder management, and cross-functional collaboration skills.
Ability to manage multiple categories in a dynamic retail or wholesale environment.
ai_core_responsibilities: Lead and manage merchandising and buying activities for commercial kitchen ware, including assortment planning and supplier management. Conduct market analysis to identify opportunities for sales growth and optimize product mix.
ai_requirements_summary: A Bachelor's Degree is required along with 7-10 years of experience in merchandising and supplier relationships. Strong analytical skills and expertise in buying and merchandising are essential.

title: Billing Specialist
description_text:
Rentokil Initial
Rentokil Initial is an international business services company employing 64,800 colleagues across 90 countries.
We strive to protect people and enhance lives, for example by controlling pests, and improving hygiene. We are experts in the fields we operate in, investing in training, science, innovation and technology. Listening and acting on feedback is part of our culture to support colleagues and our customers. Rentokil Initial regards equality and fairness as a fundamental right of all of its colleagues. We live our values of Service, Relationships and Teamwork which were identified by our colleagues across the world.

Awards
Rentokil InitiaI Philippines is officially ranked in the top three most engaged places to work in the Philippines, as part of the annual Korn Ferry Employee Engagement Awards 2018.
Philippine Best Employer Brand Awards 2019.


Find out more on careers.rentokil-initial.com
Your day-to-day responsibilities will include:
Ensure achievement of monthly DSO Budget and other KPI targets.
Manage Accounts Receivable balance and contact customers before balances become overdue to confirm payments
Responsible in identifying slow moving accounts and escalate delinquent debts for case management
Monitor customer credit limits
Acquire sufficient information to assess the credit worthiness of new clients
Monitor Field Collectors performance and productivity
In-charge of the preparation of Reminder Letters
Ensure all Customer complaints & inquiries are acted as a matter of urgency and importance following the company SOPs and 2/24 promise
To maintain good business relationship and thus improve customer retention and DSO
Responsible for the preparation of billings
Monitor and oversee respective billing portfolio assigned.
Respond to customers concerns.
Prepares necessary billing reconciliation (matching of PO) and completion of needed attachments.
Performs other tasks as may be required.
Do you have what it takes? If you want to be considered for this role you will need:
Candidates must possess at least a Bachelor’s/College Degree 
At least 1-2 year(s) of work related experience.
Preferably with experience related to the job is required.
Must have strong organizational and multitasking skills.
Can work in a fast-paced environment.
Self-motivated, proactive and team oriented person who can focus on achieving common goals.
Client service and quality oriented mindset.
With good communication skills and can deal with different kinds of customers.
Preferably can start immediately.

title: Quality Control / Quality Assurance Associate
description_text:
Clinigen is at the forefront of delivering critical medicines and support to healthcare professionals and patients. As part of our ongoing commitment to excellence, we are seeking a Quality Control / Quality Assurance Associate to join our esteemed team in Singapore. In this pivotal role, you will be instrumental in upholding our quality management standards as we expand our operations in the Southeast Asia region.
Your primary responsibilities will include: 
Overseeing documentation processes for our Quality Management System (QMS)/electronic QMS (eQMS)
Facilitating product releases to ensure compliance with established quality standards
Conducting vendor and customer approvals and validations
Providing support for secondary packaging processes
Assisting with validation activities to maintain operational efficacy
Collaborating with warehouse and cleanroom facilities to ensure adherence to quality parameters
The ideal candidate will possess the following qualifications and skills: 
A tertiary qualification in Science, Pharmacy, or a relevant technical discipline, or equivalent professional experience
Minimum of 2 years of experience in a quality assurance or quality control role; experience within a warehouse and cleanroom environment in the Healthcare, Pharmaceutical, or Food industries is highly desirable
Familiarity with Good Distribution Practice (GDP) and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) quality management systems
Exceptional communication skills, both verbal and written
The ability to cultivate collaborative relationships with cross-functional team members
Strong organizational and time management skills, with a focus on detail-oriented execution
A commitment to excellence in administrative and documentation practices
A proactive attitude, with a willingness to contribute positively to our mission of enhancing patient care
In return for your valuable contributions and commitment to our organization, we are pleased to offer you an attractive and comprehensive benefits package that includes:
A competitive monthly salary that reflects your experience, skills, and the industry standards, ensuring that you are fairly compensated for your hard work and dedication.
A performance bonus designed to reward exceptional performance and achievements, providing you with additional financial incentives as you excel in your role.
Generous annual leave of 20 days, allowing you the opportunity to take time off for rest, relaxation, and personal pursuits, promoting a healthy work-life balance.
Ongoing professional development opportunities, including training programs, workshops, and access to resources that will help you enhance your skills and advance your career within our organization.

title: Sales Development Lead
description_text:
We’re hiring our first Sales Development Lead to own and refine top-of-funnel pipeline at an early-stage B2B startup selling to professional services firms (starting with legal and finance).
This role is about creating real, high-quality sales conversations in a relationship-driven, trust-heavy market. You’ll work closely with the founders and our Founding GTM Lead to refine who we target, how we reach them, and what “qualified pipeline” actually means for Caddi.
This is an SDR role in execution, but a lead role in scope. You’ll be refining and owning our sales development motion—outbound strategy, messaging, qualification, and handoff. If successful, this role is expected to grow into Head of Sales Development, with responsibility for hiring and leading the SDR team.
We’re looking for someone who has spent time earlier in their career doing outbound or SDR-style work and wants to take ownership of improving and scaling a function—not just working a queue.
Success in this role is measured by pipeline quality, learning velocity, and repeatability.

What You’ll Do 
- Partner Closely With Sales & Founders
Work directly with founders and the Founding GTM Lead to generate qualified pipeline 
Own early discovery and qualification conversations 
Ensure clean, high-signal handoffs to AEs 
Translate objections and buyer feedback into better targeting and messaging 
- Own Outbound & Pipeline Creation
Run outbound prospecting 
Research accounts and contacts 
Execute thoughtful, personalized outreach (email, LinkedIn, warm intros) 
Book and qualify discovery conversations 
Confidently disqualify poor-fit leads 
- Refine the Sales Development Motion
Help refine ICPs, personas, and targeting criteria 
Test and iterate on outbound messaging and sequences 
Document what works and turn it into repeatable playbooks 
Create a clear learning loop between sales, founders, and product 
- Lay the Foundation for a Scaled SDR Team
Establish what “good pipeline” looks like for Caddi 
Help define future SDR roles and processes 
Prepare the ground to hire SDR #2 and beyond
3–5 years of experience in Sales Development, Business Development, or outbound-focused roles 
Hands-on experience doing outbound prospecting and qualification 
Experience at a Seed or Series A startup, working closely with AEs and founders in an early-stage or fast-growing environment 
Comfort operating in an early, evolving GTM environment and refining existing processes 
Strong written and verbal communication skills 
Thoughtful, professional approach to outbound (not spray-and-pray) 
Bias toward action, testing, and learning
Familiarity with modern sales development tooling (e.g. Apollo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, HubSpot, Clay, or similar) 
Nice to have:
Experience selling to professional services (law, finance, consulting, etc.) 
Early sales hire with meaningful ownership and influence 
Clear path to Head of Sales Development 
Competitive base salary ($60,000–$80,000) plus commission tied to performance 
Meaningful early-stage equity 
Medical (platinum PPO), dental, and vision — 100% covered for employees; subsidized dependents 
In-person, fast-paced environment with a technical, ambitious, mission-driven team
ai_core_responsibilities: The Sales Development Lead will own and refine the top-of-funnel pipeline, focusing on creating high-quality sales conversations. This includes partnering with founders, running outbound prospecting, and refining the sales development motion.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 3-5 years of experience in Sales Development or outbound-focused roles, preferably in a startup environment. Strong communication skills and a thoughtful approach to outbound sales are essential.

title: Inbound Appointment Setter
description_text:
Join Pitchbox as Our Inbound Appointment Setter
About Pitchbox
Pitchbox makes AI-powered software that helps marketing, digital PR, and sales teams scale their prospecting and outreach. We've been growing for over a decade, serve customers across 57 countries, and still operate with a small, driven team where your work has direct impact on company growth. If you thrive in environments where things move quickly and every conversation counts, you'll fit right in.
About the Role
We're looking for a full-time Inbound Appointment Setter to join our team at our Warminster, Pennsylvania office. Whether you're a recent grad looking to break into tech, making a career switch from hospitality, recruiting, or customer service, or already in sales and ready for a role with real growth potential, this role was built for you.
You'll be the first voice prospects hear after they raise their hand, responding to demo requests, qualifying leads from our marketing campaigns, and booking meetings with our sales team. This is where you'll master the fundamentals: understanding our product, learning to qualify leads, and developing the communication skills that make top salespeople.
This is a position with a clear career path. Successful Appointment Setters get promoted to Sales Development Representative (SDR), and from there to Account Executive. If you're ready to grow, we'll give you the opportunities to prove yourself.
What You'll Do
Respond promptly to inbound leads from website forms, live chat, and marketing campaigns
Qualify prospects by understanding their needs and confirming fit with Pitchbox
Schedule demos and discovery calls with Account Executives
Maintain accurate records of all interactions in our CRM
Follow up with leads who aren't ready to book immediately
Collaborate with sales and marketing to improve lead quality and conversion
Meet or exceed weekly appointment-setting targets
Learn the product inside and out. This knowledge will fuel your growth

What You Bring
Strong verbal communication and active listening skills
Comfortable handling high volumes of calls, emails, and chats
Self-motivated with strong organizational skills
Ability to qualify leads quickly and ask the right questions
Professional, friendly demeanor. You make a great first impression
Reliable and punctual
You're not looking for "just a job." You want a career path you can grow into
Nice to Have
Previous experience in B2B sales or SaaS environment
Experience with CRM software (HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar)
Familiarity with sales engagement tools
Background in customer service or call center work
What We Offer
Full-time position with Competitive Compensation and Uncapped Commission
Career Growth: Clear promotion path: Appointment Setter → SDR → Account Executive
Health insurance, PTO, and vacation benefits
Work in our Warminster, PA office during business hours (9-5)
Learn directly from the founders. You'll get mentorship and visibility that's impossible at a bigger company
Hands-on training and continuous sales training as you progress through each stage of your career
Be part of a small team where your contributions are noticed and rewarded

Tell us why you want to build a career in tech sales and what makes you the right person for this role. We're reviewing applications on a rolling basis.

🚀 Apply today for immediate consideration! 

This role is sometimes referred to as: Business Development Representative (BDR), Sales Development Representative (SDR), Inside Sales Representative, or Account Development Representative (ADR).

title: Product Practice Analyst
description_text:
Be part of something that matters
Australian Payments Plus AP+ is shaping the future of payments in Australia. We bring together the country’s key payment systems to create smarter, safer and more connected experiences for businesses, government and people every day.
Our work spans real time payments, secure bill payments, digital identity, QR payments, open wallets and Australia’s domestic debit network. This is a big moment for payments and you can be part of it.
Life at AP+
We are one team, working towards one clear purpose. We value curiosity, kindness and impact. You will be encouraged to speak up, try new ideas and learn as you go.
We care about your growth, but also about balance. There is space here for flexibility, friendships and fun, alongside meaningful work. Our values guide us every day. Lead with heart. Learn for tomorrow. Live our legacy.
The role
The purpose of this role is to support AP+’s Product function by analysing how product-related work is performed end-to-end, identifying risks, inefficiencies and opportunities, and recommending practical improvements that strengthen governance, consistency and delivery outcomes. The role partners with stakeholders to document and improve processes, embed fit-for-purpose methods and tools, and use data-driven insights to help the Product team operate more effectively and continuously improve.
What you will do
Investigate product practice situations with some complexity/ambiguity to identify problems, opportunities and root causes. 
Analyse existing product practice processes end-to-end and recommend improvements to efficiency, effectiveness, quality and control. 
Develop and maintain clear product framework practices and proceedures documentation (e.g., maps, procedures, standards, work instructions) in line with agreed practices. 
Identify and assess product framework process risks and contribute to improvement recommendations and mitigation actions. 
Support the adoption and effective use of product methods and tools; provide guidance to stakeholders on standards, policies and best practice. 
Tailor product processes to meet practical team needs while ensuring alignment to established standards and governance requirements. 
Use data and stakeholder feedback to generate insights, track outcomes, and support evidence-based decisions on process changes. 
Assist with implementing improvements, including change communication, training/support materials, and monitoring early effectiveness. 
Review and continuously improve how methods/tools are applied, proposing refinements based on lessons learned and performance outcomes. 
Engage collaboratively with stakeholders (under direction where required) to gather requirements, validate findings, and drive alignment on improvements 
What you bring
Relevant tertiary qualification in business, commerce, finance, IT, data/analytics, operations, or a related discipline (or equivalent experience). 
Certifications in process improvement or analysis (e.g., Lean/Six Sigma, BPMN) are advantageous. 
Experience analysing and improving framework processes/proceedures within product, technology or operations environments, translating findings into practical improvements. 
Demonstrated stakeholder engagement experience to gather requirements, document processes, and support adoption of improved ways of working, methods and tools. 
Working knowledge of process analysis and improvement techniques (e.g., process mapping, root cause analysis, controls/hand-offs) and basic risk concepts. 
Practical knowledge of data analysis and reporting to generate insights, define data requirements, and measure the effectiveness of process changes 
Why join us
You will work on meaningful products that impact millions of Australians. You will be trusted to contribute, improve things and grow your career. And you will do it alongside people who care about the work and each other.
What happens next
If this sounds like you, we would love to hear from you. Apply now and our Talent team will be in touch within two weeks.
If you need support during the application process, please contact recruitment@auspayplus.com.au. You are welcome to share your pronouns at any stage.
Please note AP+ is not partnering with recruitment agencies for this role
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves analyzing product-related work to identify risks and inefficiencies, recommending improvements, and documenting processes. It also includes supporting the adoption of product methods and tools while engaging with stakeholders to drive alignment on improvements.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a relevant tertiary qualification or equivalent experience, with certifications in process improvement being advantageous. Experience in analyzing and improving processes within product or technology environments is required, along with stakeholder engagement skills.

title: Customer Success Consultant
locations_alt_raw: North Sydney, New South Wales, Australia
description_text:
Come shape the future of education with us.
At Compass, we love working with schools and strive to deliver new innovations, functions, and process improvements as new technology becomes available. Our services are specifically designed to improve not only the school process but also community culture and student learning outcomes.
Compass is a product that genuinely provides students, teachers, and parents with an improved educational experience. You can leave at the end of each day knowing you made a difference to a student's education or helped make a staff member’s day just that little bit better.
Our school management ecosystem is now used in more than 5,000 schools across Australia and beyond. We’re joined by over 300+ members of the Compass team, delivering smart school management solutions around the world.
About the Role & Team
As a Customer Success Consultant, you will be working with a number of our priority clients to ensure their satisfaction and retention in the Compass product. You will work with the independent schools to provide exceptional service and support resulting in increased retention and ARR, as well as resulting in advocacy and promotion of the Compass product to future potential clients. 
What you’ll do:
Manage overall workflow for implementation, training, and account management of assigned signature schools. 
Coordinate the implementation of new modules with customers. 
Complete onsite/remote training sessions & account management visits.
Ensure the highest level of service for your portfolio of clients. 
Ensure all schools in your portfolio are active promoters of the platform.
Build relationships with school leadership to ensure product rollout success. 
Provide clear notes and feedback and escalate customer concerns appropriately.
Provide phone, email, and in-person support to customers when needed. 
Escalate concerns appropriately to ensure maximum customer satisfaction.
About You
We are looking for people who have a passion for creativity and problem-solving. Our clients are constantly giving us new challenges to work through and resolve so excellent customer service skills are a must. We’re also looking for people who celebrate diversity and thrive in a collaborative environment.
Essential:
A demonstrated service-orientated approach and are a motivated and enthusiastic self-starter.
The ability to thrive under pressure. 
Strong verbal and written communication skills. 
Strong attention to detail and ability to see tasks through to conclusion.
Ability to build positive professional relationships with clients and educational staff.
Desirable:
Comprehensive knowledge and confidence utilising the Compass platform.
Education experience, whether that is in a Primary or Secondary school teaching setting or prior experience as a Customer Success Consultant.
What’s in it for you?
We come together with our unique strengths to work towards a common purpose - shaping the future of education. When you join our team, you will collaborate with individuals who are passionate, driven, and open to embracing change in a fast-paced environment.
A flexible working culture.
Learning & Development opportunities - we want you to grow and get the most out of your role!
Parental Leave Program for both primary and secondary carers.
24/7 access to our Employment Assistance Program (EAP) through the means of face-to-face, livechat and phone support.
It is a really exciting time to join Compass. We are growing and need people who want to grow with us. 
We value individuals who are self-motivated, adaptable, and eager to contribute to the success of our company. Compass is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We embrace and celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.
Prior To Commencing Employment, You Require:
A valid Employee Working With Children Check
Verification of unrestricted work rights in Australia, e.g., citizenship, passport, or birth certificate
A successful National Police Check
A valid Drivers Licence

title: Junior Legal Counsel
locations_alt_raw: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
description_text:
QCP is Asia's leading digital asset partner, empowering clients to seamlessly integrate digital assets into their portfolios.
We offer a comprehensive range of solutions - from spot on/off ramping and fixed income strategies to vanilla options and bespoke exotics.
Driven by the vision to be the most trusted partner in digital asset markets, we provide innovative solutions that make digital assets a core component of every portfolio, balance sheet, and treasury.
Since our founding in 2017, we have witnessed the potential of digital assets to transform financial markets and the world at large. We exist at the centre of change, successfully navigating three market cycles and bridging institutional and crypto ecosystems.
We work to build trusted partnerships, putting client success and care at the heart of everything that we do.
Responsibilities:
Conduct legal research and prepare internal legal memorandums and regulatory updates.
Assist in drafting, reviewing and negotiating commercial contracts and legal documentation.
Review and maintain internal policies, guidelines, templates and standard operating procedures.
Assist with day-to-day legal operations including document management, internal stakeholder queries, corporate secretarial matters and other administrative duties.
Support cross-functional projects, new business initiatives and ad hoc legal matters.
Assist in coordination with external counsel.
Bachelor’s degree in Law (LLB or equivalent) from a recognised university.
0–2 years of relevant experience; prior exposure to or interest in financial regulations or digital assets is a plus.
Strong sense of responsibility, accountability, and professionalism.
Proactive, resourceful, and willing to take ownership of tasks, including routine and administrative work.
Positive, collaborative attitude with the ability to work well in a close-knit team and support cross-functional stakeholders.
Willingness to learn, adapt, and handle unfamiliar tasks in a fast-paced and evolving industry.
Good communication, research and analytical skills.
The Environment We Offer
As a growing firm with a tightly-knit team, we respect and listen to all our employees. You will get the chance to make an impact by having your voice heard by everyone, including the management.
Our employees enjoy a high level of autonomy at work. We focus on substance, not form - as long as you can perform, you will be recognized and rewarded. We are also dedicated to supporting our staff and ensuring they develop holistically to maximize their potential in the long- term.
We also provide flexible working arrangement as required and a casual and fun environment to boot!

title: Senior Specialist, Channel Marketing (Online)
description_text:
Moomoo is more than just a global investment and trading platform—we’re a fast-growing force in the wealth-tech space, committed to transforming the way people invest. Since our founding in 2018, we've rapidly expanded across Singapore, Australia, Japan, Canada, and Malaysia, now empowering over 26 million users worldwide.
In Singapore, Moomoo Financial Singapore Pte. Ltd. is licensed and regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (Licence No. CMS101000). In 2022, we became the first digital brokerage to earn all five SGX Group memberships across the securities and derivatives markets—a testament to our dedication to excellence and innovation.
But what truly sets us apart is our mission: to democratise investing and make smart, data-driven financial decisions accessible to everyone. We’re here to level the playing field—bringing powerful tools, real-time insights, and intuitive technology to empower investors at every stage of their journey. 
Joining Moomoo means being part of a purpose-driven team that’s not only reshaping the future of investing—but also making wealth-building inclusive, informed, and inspiring. If you're excited about driving impact at the intersection of finance and technology, there's never been a better time to join us.
Senior Specialist, Channel Marketing (KOL & Partnership)
We are looking for a highly motivated Senior Specialist, Channel Marketing (Online) to join our dynamic channel marketing team. This role will be responsible for leading and managing online campaigns/initiatives across KOL and partnership channels to boost client acquisition and brand growth.
Responsibilities
Develop, execute and optimise channel specific initiatives to drive user acquisition, growth and brand awareness in Singapore.
Collaborate with cross-function teams to ensure the effective implementation of KOL/KOC and online partnership campaigns.
Support the team to define relevant growth and marketing strategies, translating KPIs into actionable plans and optimising performance based on data insights. 
Track and analyze channel and campaign performance, identifying trends and coordinating with relevant teams to refine the initiatives.
Stay updated on industry trends and best practices, leveraging insights to explore new opportunities and enhance campaign effectiveness.
Support any other relevant channel marketing initiatives where necessary. 
Our Values 
Integrity 
Integrity Story: Say No to Super High Leverage
We hold fast to our value of integrity and attach great importance to user experience and risk management. Unlike some other brokerages, we don’t try to attract users’ attention by claiming to enable super high-leverage financing for IPO subscriptions, which quickly reaches the limit.
Efficiency
Efficiency Story: Promise Made to a User on New Year's Eve
On February 11, 2021, Chinese New Year's Eve, a user wrote a post in the Community at around 8 AM and received Leaf's reply just 8 minutes later. According to the user's suggestion, we issued a letter to shareholders after releasing our annual report.
Service
Service Story: Providing Excellent Services and Undertaking Our Responsibilities
After Ant Group’s IPO was suspended, we refunded all subscribers’ subscription fees and margin interest, bearing all the costs ourselves.
Excellence
Excellence Story: Leaf Opens Our App Over 30 Times a Day
According to statistics, Leaf opens our app over 30 times a day on average. He repeatedly checks every detail. While normal users might just enter a trading page and stop there, Leaf taps some seemingly boring or repetitive elements on the page. He continually assesses the app’s loading speed and visual effects.
Please note that only short-listed candidates will be contacted.
Requirements
Strong ability to develop and execute marketing strategies, optimize content, and drive customer acquisition.
Proven project management skills, with experience in cross-functional collaboration and task prioritization.
Data-driven mindset, with the ability to analyze key performance metrics and make informed marketing decisions.
Proactive and adaptable, with a passion for staying ahead of industry trends.
Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field.
Minimum 3 years of experience in marketing campaign planning and management, preferably within the investment, trading, or financial sectors.
Strong interest in investments and trading; candidates with personal investing or trading experience will be highly regarded.
English speaking and Fluency in Mandarin is an added advantage
Working at Moomoo, you will get 
Be a part of the world’s leading digital brokerage firm where growth and career development opportunities are endless.
Enjoy a competitive total compensation package that truly values your skills and contributions.
Comprehensive insurance coverage for employees and dependents to provide you a peace of mind 
Fun loving and diverse work environment that hosts various team-bonding programs and company events 
Business casual work attire everyday
And Much More!: Discover even more perks and benefits as we take you through the hiring process!

title: Sr. QA Test Engineer (REMOTE)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Software Test Engineer. In this role, you will contribute to ensuring software quality through advanced automation techniques. Collaborating with cross-functional teams, your expertise in Python will be essential as you design and implement robust automated testing strategies. Join a diverse team of professionals from around the world, and bring your innovative spirit to help deliver high-quality software solutions.
\n

Accountabilities
Design, develop, and maintain robust and scalable test automation frameworks and test suites using Python.
Implement automated testing strategies for APIs and ML/AI models, ensuring accuracy, performance, and reliability.
Collaborate with developers, product managers, and other QA engineers to define test strategies and ensure comprehensive test coverage.
Analyze complex software systems to identify opportunities for automation to improve testing efficiency.
Execute automated test scripts, report defects, and work closely with development teams for timely resolution.
Monitor and maintain test automation environments, ensuring stability and reliability.
Mentor and guide junior QA engineers in best practices for test automation and API testing.
Continuously evaluate and adopt new tools and technologies to enhance testing processes.
Participate in code reviews and provide constructive feedback to improve code quality.
Contribute to the overall QA strategy, driving continuous improvement initiatives within the team.

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).
Minimum of 5 years of experience in software quality assurance, with a focus on automation testing.
Proficiency in Python for writing API automation.
Experience using Playwright and Python for performing UI testing.
Experience testing Web Service APIs with manual and automation tools (Postman, JMeter, SoapUI, etc.).
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work in a remote, team-oriented environment.
Experience with Agile/Scrum methodologies is a plus.
Certification in software testing (e.g., ISTQB) is desirable but not required.

Benefits
Your growth is our growth. We invest in your future with paid training and other professional opportunities.
We’re industry innovators at the forefront of change, equipped with the latest technologies.
Work from the location and schedule that suits you best - your time zone, your preferred hours.
Flexibility and freedom - travel and work from anywhere with a stable internet connection.
A reliable workload with enough tasks to keep you engaged and challenged.
Recognition and reward through annual pay increases for good performance.

title: Sr. Sales and Marketing Assistant Manager (REMOTE)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Assistant Manager, Sales and Marketing. In this role, you will have the opportunity to drive new business opportunities while managing and maximizing profits for existing accounts. The position demands a proactive approach in coordinating with both local and international clients, while ensuring the seamless operation of export procedures. You will support the sales and marketing team, develop key relationships, and contribute to achieving targeted budgets. This is a pivotal role in shaping the future of our partner's sales strategies in a dynamic environment.

New account acquisitions are both local and international.

Developing and managing relationships with key customer accounts and carriers.
Coordinating with shippers and recovering outstanding payments within agreed credit terms.
Preparing and conducting sales and marketing presentations and proposals.

Resolving contractual and commercial disputes.
Supporting the sales and marketing team.
Achieving and exceeding individual set budgets.

A diploma or degree related to Transport or Logistics & Supply Chain Management with 5+ years of experience in the freight forwarding and logistics industry.
Experience in a business development or sales position with an existing customer base.
Good presentation and negotiation skills.
Proven track record of success in business development or a sales role within the freight forwarding industry.
Knowledge in Sales & Marketing, Sea freight, Air freight Operations, and Export Documentation.
Proficiency in computer applications such as MS Office, MS Word, and MS PowerPoint.

title: Sr. Strategic Finance Manager (Remote)
description_text:

title: Sr. Marketing Analytics Specialist (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Marketing Analytics Specialist. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming marketing data into actionable insights. Your work will help build dashboards that enable teams to understand performance across multiple channels, thereby influencing strategic decisions. You will be essential in optimizing performance and driving the success of marketing campaigns. If you have a growth mindset and a passion for data, we want to hear from you!
\n

Accountabilities
Build and maintain KPI dashboards in Looker Studio from scratch
Integrate data from Shopify, Klaviyo, Postscript, Google Analytics, Google Ads, and Meta Ads
Track and visualize key marketing metrics such as CAC, ROAS, LTV, AOV, conversion rates, and campaign performance
Evolve dashboards as new products, campaigns, and marketing channels launch
Partner with the Head of Growth to define KPIs, reporting cadence, and visualization needs
Document dashboard logic, data sources, and calculations for long-term scalability
Perform ad hoc analyses to identify trends, insights, and optimization opportunities

Requirements
3+ years of experience in marketing analytics, data visualization, or related field
Proficiency in Looker Studio (Google Data Studio)
Experience with DTC/eCommerce data from Shopify, Klaviyo, Postscript, or paid ad platforms
Strong understanding of performance metrics: CAC, ROAS, LTV, AOV, conversion rates
Exceptional attention to detail and data accuracy
Clear communicator capable of explaining insights to non-technical stakeholders
Comfortable in a fast-paced startup environment and managing multiple priorities
Ability to work US hours

Benefits
Work-from-Home Forever: Enjoy a stable remote setup
Bonuses for anniversary, referrals, attendance, and performance
Paid Time Off: 7 holiday PTO + 6 annual PTO days
Prepaid Health Card for peace of mind
Parental Leave
Competitive compensation
Flexibility and autonomy in a fully remote role

title: Sr. Instructional Designer (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Instructional Designer - REMOTE. This role plays a crucial part in developing high-quality courses and curricula focused on medical coding and billing. As part of the Education/Product team, the ideal candidate will blend instructional design expertise with collaborative strategies to create effective and engaging learning experiences. The position requires a deep understanding of instructional design principles, adult learning strategies, and digital content development in a fast-paced, agile environment. A strong proficiency in Articulate Storyline is essential, along with demonstrated experience in integrating AI technologies to enhance educational workflows and learner engagement.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with SMEs to assess learning needs and design instructional strategies.
Create engaging course content for online and hybrid delivery.
Develop comprehensive storyboards outlining course structure and flow.
Utilize expert knowledge of Articulate Storyline for high-quality learning experiences.
Develop assessments that measure learning outcomes and performance improvement.
Translate complex medical concepts into accessible, learner-friendly content.
Manage end-to-end course development processes, including timelines and quality control.
Ensure compliance with accessibility and Section 508 standards.
Conduct iterative quality assurance checks and apply feedback for improvement.
Maintain accurate documentation for instructional materials and processes.
Use rapid prototyping and agile methodologies in content development.
Contribute to continuous improvement through learner feedback and program evaluations.
Collaborate with diverse teams while working U.S. hours.
Adapt curricula for international learners while preserving instructional integrity.
Explore and integrate emerging technologies to enhance instructional efficiency.
Perform additional duties and special projects as assigned.

Requirements
Bachelor's degree in Instructional Design, Educational Technology, or related field.
Master’s degree in Instructional Design or Learning Sciences preferred.
Minimum 5 years of experience in instructional design for adult learning.
Demonstrated proficiency in instructional design models (e.g., ADDIE, SAM).
Expert in eLearning tools such as Articulate 360 and Adobe Creative Suite.
Extensive experience with Learning Management Systems (LMS).
Knowledge of accessibility and 508 compliance requirements.
Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
Proven ability to manage multiple projects in a remote setting.
Strong organizational and project management skills.
Ability to work collaboratively with diverse stakeholders.
Experience translating technical or medical information into instructional content.
Experience integrating AI technologies to enhance educational content.
Medical coding or healthcare education background is a plus.

Benefits
Flexible remote working conditions.
Opportunities for professional development and continuous learning.
Collaborative and supportive team environment.
Exposure to emerging technologies and innovative practices.
Diverse and inclusive workplace culture.

In this role, you will be instrumental in supporting Senior Level Executive Service members, ensuring the smooth operation of their offices. You'll be tasked with high-level administrative duties that directly impact the efficiency of government operations. This is an opportunity to work with a dynamic team, where your contributions will lead to meaningful outcomes for stakeholders. Embrace a challenging environment where your organizational skills and attention to detail will shine as you coordinate essential tasks and communications.
\n

Accountabilities
Perform routine executive-level tasks such as scheduling, drafting memos, and creating briefing materials.
Take creative direction and work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced team environment.
Manage and de-conflict Executive and Principal Director calendars.
Handle electronic action binders via MS Teams for Executives and Principal Directors.
Coordinate meetings and conference calls, including scheduling and logistics.
Arrange travel for Executives and Principal Directors, both domestic and international.
Ensure the appropriate routing and submission of high-level documents.
Maintain comprehensive records in accordance with management directives.
Carry out ad-hoc administrative duties as requested.

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree with 7 years of relevant professional experience (or equivalent).
Experience supporting federal Senior Executive Service members.
Detail-oriented with experience in task tracking, meeting coordination, and travel preparation.
Ability to generate reports and develop briefing materials.
Strong communication skills, both orally and in writing.
Must have an active SECRET security clearance.
US Citizenship is required.

Benefits
Comprehensive benefits package including 401k matching (4%).
Paid Time Off (PTO) starting at 3 weeks, increasing to 5 weeks with tenure.
Health, dental, and vision insurance.
Short- and long-term disability coverage.
Flexible Spending Account (FSA) options.
Paid parental leave (4 weeks).
11 paid holidays, including your birthday off.
Fitness and cell phone reimbursements.
Annual team building events and monthly social activities.
A collaborative company culture with regular check-ins for professional growth.

title: Sr. Clinical Review Nurse (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Clinical Review Nurse - REMOTE. In this role, you will leverage your nursing expertise to make a significant impact in the healthcare sector. By focusing on denied insurance claims and crafting compelling clinical arguments, you will empower healthcare facilities to recover lost revenues. This position allows you to work fully remotely, offering flexibility while contributing to the optimization of healthcare revenue cycles. Join a dedicated team that prioritizes patient care and strives to create a supportive environment for both employees and patients alike.
\n

Accountabilities
Analyze denied insurance claims and complete medical records.
Apply clinical reasoning, national criteria (InterQual/Milliman), and best practices to determine if appeals are justified.
Draft persuasive, medically sound appeal letters that clearly support the need for treatment or services.
Collaborate with the legal team to ensure appeals are compelling and complete.
Stay informed on healthcare regulations, payer trends, and clinical updates.

Requirements
RN License (required)
5+ years of acute hospital experience (required)
Certification in Case Management, Legal Nurse Consulting, or Coding is a plus.
Knowledge and experience with national clinical criteria applied in case management including InterQual and Milliman standards.
Experience and knowledge of managed care contracts, account receivables and revenue cycle functions (preferred).
Experience and success in medical record chart review and appealing managed care denials (preferred).

Benefits
100% Remote: Work from anywhere while making a tangible difference.
Competitive Salary: $65,000–$85,000 per year (commensurate with experience).
Robust Benefits Package: 401(k) with corporate match, comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance.
Work-Life Balance: Flexible schedule and paid time off.
Career Growth: Access to professional development, mentorship, and upward mobility within a thriving company.
Additional Perks: Life insurance and performance-based bonuses.

title: Sr. C++/QT/3D Engineer (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote C++/QT/3D Engineer. This role will provide you with the opportunity to make a significant impact in a fully remote environment, working on cutting-edge applications. You will be at the forefront of innovation, leveraging your expertise in C++, QT, and 3D geometry to contribute to our global projects. Join a diverse team that values growth and offers a flexible work schedule tailored to your needs. Your skills and passion will be key to our success and growth.
\n

Accountabilities
Develop and maintain software applications using modern C++.
Implement 3D geometry and mesh processing algorithms.
Create user interfaces for desktop applications leveraging QT.
Integrate 3D scanning hardware and develop related workflows.
Optimize performance and ensure efficiency in software design.
Collaborate with a global team on innovative software solutions.

Requirements
5+ years of software development experience in C++.
Strong proficiency in 3D geometry and related algorithms.
Experience in building desktop applications with QT (Qt Widgets and/or Qt Quick).
Knowledge of 3D scanning processes and hardware integration.
Familiarity with algorithms like mesh stitching and ICP.
Experience with 3D processing libraries such as PCL, MeshLib, or Open3D.
Ability to optimize software performance, including multi-threading.

title: Sr. Communications Sustainment Analyst (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Communications Sustainment Analyst. This role is crucial for maintaining effective communication during the sustainment phase of the VA Electronic Health Record modernization. By collaborating with various stakeholders, including deployment sites and leadership, the analyst will play a pivotal role in ensuring operational continuity and stakeholder alignment. The impact of this position extends to effectively managing change communications, enhancing the user experience, and shaping the future of health IT services for veterans. This position offers the opportunity to work remotely while contributing to significant initiatives.
\n

Accountabilities
Lead and coordinate sustainment communications for VA sites, including EHR system alerts, newsletters, and SharePoint content.
Support the transition from deployment to sustainment, ensuring sites are prepared for long-term management.
Collaborate with VA, Oracle Health, and site communications teams to align messages with EHRM-IO objectives.
Facilitate meetings, manage action items, and report on communications metrics.
Support briefings and respond to inquiries from VA leadership, Congressional stakeholders, and media.
Define, document, and refine sustainment communications processes and provide feedback on deliverables.
Manage weekly Joint Sustainment and Adoption Advisory Board (JSAAB) notifications and ad hoc communications requests.

Requirements
Experience with project life cycle management, change management, or enterprise communications.
Strong stakeholder communication and risk management skills.
Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field or 10+ years of equivalent experience.
Preferred: VA or EHR system experience, federal IT communications, and sustainment support.

Paid time off
